p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, stable posture.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained consistent turgidity throughout the 5-day sequence; no significant leaf drop or wilting observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: Healthy. Leaf color remains deep green with no signs of chlorosis or necrosis.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, central position, two primary leaves with developing secondary pair.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The plant shows recovery from the initial wilting seen in the earliest image. The leaves have regained structural integrity.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Stable. The recovery of leaf posture indicates successful hydration management despite the A5 sensor failure.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit scale anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The leaves remain stable relative to the rabbit anchor. No significant growth or decline in leaf surface area.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable. Tip necrosis remains localized and has not progressed.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen, located near the rim of the p2/p4 shared pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remains in a dormant/slow-growth state. No change in position relative to the pot rim.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Stable. No signs of distress or desiccation.

biome_observations.soil_surface: The white granular material (perlite/additive) is present across the soil surface, confirming user-applied care.
biome_observations.moisture_indicators: Soil texture appears consistent with the recent power-cut recovery and cooling trial; no signs of surface crusting or excessive dryness.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t5: Introduction of white granular material observed in T-5.
temporal_deltas.t5_to_current: The soil surface remains stable; the plant specimens show no negative reaction to the cooling trial (25C).
temporal_deltas.notable_change: The transition from the earliest image to T-5 shows a clear intervention (addition of white material) which has remained stable through the current timestamp.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of equilibrium. The cooling trial (25C) has not induced visible stress, and the plants appear to be adapting well to the current environmental parameters.
vision_report.anomalies: None. The white material on the soil is identified as a successful outcome of user care (per instructions).
vision_report.narrative_description: The garden is stable. Following the power cut and subsequent cooling trial, the plants show no signs of physiological distress. The Mexican Mint (p2) has successfully recovered from its earlier drooping state. The white granular material on the soil surface is a confirmed positive intervention. The environment is currently well-regulated.
